M1 gives 10% RES PEN when using One's Path or Earth Shaker, 10% Sheer DMG% when using certain skills; using Crushing Peaks extends stun duration by 2s on stunned target, which can be good for short stun duration target but can lose out another stun when used with Dialyn, making it situational.

High-level gameplay for Banyue requires executing his Perfect Cancel well (adding Basic/EX input at the correct time while another animation is running) to fit in the most DMG. Each version of EX skill has different animation length and scaling, so what combo to use during a stun duration is dependant on the enemy. Focus on fitting the free EX skills and an enhanced EX skill in stun time.
Currently, it's recommended to loop EX: Earth Shaker when out of stun as it has the highest scaling but of course it depend on the flow of battle. Sub-Optimal gameplay can still clear end-game.

builds.mindscapes:
M1: Provides significant QoL, enabling Caesar to apply her Shield when entering the field without activating it (on a 300s Cooldown), while further applying an All-Attribute RES reduction to nearby enemies.
M2: Further increases the ATK buff her Shield provides, by up to 500 ATK more. It also slightly increases Caesar's Energy Generation Rate by 10%, which increases the Energy gain from all sources.
builds.other_notes:
Parries, Block Counters, and Retaliatory Attacks after Assists and Dodge Counters are the best and fastest way to reapply Caesar's shield and buffs for the team and debuff on nearby targets.
As a Defense agent you will need to use your EX Special even when you do not have energy for EX Special to activate her block counter. Caesar is best used in short bursts to apply Daze/Rebuff. 
Reel Projector is not "worth" to level up for Caesar. Levelling a Stun engine has more value, since you can use it on a Stun unit while not using Caesar. Reel's passive effectively does nothing more than Stun engines do. Consider it an alternative to the A-Rank stun W-Engines.

builds.w_engine_notes:
Steam Oven can be preferred over Hellfire Gears in some scenarios, since ER% Passive from Hellfire Gears is only during Dialyn's off-field time which leads to less Energy total compared to Steam Oven, and less on-fielding capabilities. Steam Oven's 25.6% Impact can be up during key moments like Defensive Assists or Ultimate, while Hellfire Gears needs to use an EX or 2 for maximum Impact buff.
Hellfire Gears with higher Refinement is preferred over Steam Oven due to more Impact and more ER% regardless of said issues.
builds.mindscapes:
M1: Increases her Positive Review generation and amount gained by using her EX Special Attacks by 16%. Agents in the Overwhelmingly Positive state ignore 15% enemy All-Attribute RES.
M2: Grants an additional 20% Stun DMG Multiplier on enemies under Malicious Complaint, and the team deals +15% DMG to enemies under that effect.
M1 is a good stopping point, save M2 for later once you invest into your teams a little more.
builds.other_notes:
Dialyn's Chain-to-Ult conversion works as follows: She gains 1 Customer Complaint when an Ultimate is used or upon reaching 90 Positive Reviews. She generates 0.6 Positive Reviews per second and 7.5 per EX.
Upon reaching 90: Triggering a Chain Attack window will convert the next agent’s Chain into their Ultimate, consuming 60 Positive Reviews if done when the enemy is stunned. If her positive reviews are less than 90, she can hold BA to consume Customer Complaint, triggering a Quick Assist instead. Outside-of-stun, if Dialyn has 90+ Positive Reviews, she triggers a Chain Attack window instead of a quick assist, upgrading the next Chain Attack into an Ultimate.

builds.mindscapes:
M1 allows more stacks from your basic attack and your quivers shooting another arrow at the target, which makes him able to do a few more Dashes during stun window, and improving his on field/out of stun capability. M2 gives his own dash attacks 50% DMG buff after using Chain Attack/Ultimate. 
it's recommended to Vertically invest in supportive agent's mindscapes first (such as Dialyn, Trigger, Astra, etc.) before pulling Harumasa's mindscapes.
builds.other_notes:
Harumasa's main game plan is to fit an Ultimate and as many Enhanced Dash Attacks (EDA for short) as possible during the stun window. 
Before entering the battle, Harumasa can pre-generate 6 Electro Quivers via doing his Basic Attack strings 3 times. If there's already 6 Quivers on field, Harumasa can use a Chain Attack/EX Special to generate 6 more Quivers, which makes the previous Quivers fired to the enemy and mark it. by doing this you can save times from his aiming attack, allowing more EDAs in stun.

builds.other_notes:
Hugo has a pretty straightforward playstyle: Stun the enemy, then use double Chain Attack with Hugo, get Dark Abyss Reverb state, then EX/Ult which triggers Totalize and ends the stun state, rinse and repeat.

Along with Miyabi, Alice and Yanagi are not great partners with Velina. Alice due to no Disorders for Blade Etiquette generation, and Yanagi due to Wind's Disorder MV being very low.

builds.mindscapes:
M1: Increases the gain of Flight Feather, increases the Attribute Anomaly and Disorder DMG% of the team. (decent)
M2: Increases Vivian's Anomaly Buildup Rate, her Abloom scaling is increased and ignores 15% RES. (amazing)
M6: Further enhances Flight Feather gain rate, increases Ether DMG% and multiplicatively boost Abloom DMG without fear of Feather stacks overcap. (amazing)
M2 is the recommended stopping point for those who choose to invest in Mindscapes, but her M6 is also amazing if you want to really invest into her.
builds.other_notes:
Although Core states "per 10 point of Anomaly Proficiency", each point of AP is taken into account for her Abloom scaling.
Fire, Electric, Ice, and Physical DMG% on Vivian don't affect the respective Attribute Abloom DMG.
When swapped out during Noblewoman state, Vivian automatically converts her Flight Feather into Guard Feather. Use a Quick Assist or her Basic Attack string to quickly convert her feathers at the start of battle.

builds.mindscapes:
M1 enables YSG to immediately enter the Enlightened state when entering battle, and further amplifies her damage. (decent)
M2 allows YSG to overflow up to 6 stacks of Qingming Sword Force instead of 3, while increasing the amount of EX Special Attack: Enlightened Mind - Soaring Light that can be used while preserving the same amount of Basic Attacks that use Sword Force. EX Special: Soaring Light and Ultimate: Cleaving Heavens ignore 40% of enemy DEF. (amazing)
M2 is the recommended stopping point.
builds.other_notes:
Ye Shunguang plays around her Enlightened state, activated by getting 6 stack of Qingming Sword Force. She gains stacks through attacks, with additional points granted if you Perfect Dodge using her Special.
Full stacks can be given from the following sources: 1 upon using her EX, 6 upon using Ult outside of Enlightened state, and 3 from her Additional Ability. Excess Qingming Sword Force stacks are converted to Bearer, including those gained while in Enlightened state. Upon ending the state, those are converted back to Qingming Sword Force up to 3, so make sure to not waste your excess stacks at this point.

builds.mindscapes:
M1 - Provides 10% in-combat CR% while allowing her to start the fight with full Talisman bar. In addition to follow up attack whenever another agent deal damage to an enemy target with 6 sec Cooldown
M2 -  Ultimate/EX Special now ignore 15% Ether res and casting a decibel Ult on a stunned target, extend their stun duration for 3 sec. After Casting Talisman Ult, gain 1 cast of special EX that deal 1200% Sheer Force
M6 - Casting Ult allows you to bypass the resource cost of Talisman Ult with a cooldown of 30 sec, further more increase Yixuan Sheer Force by 20% during her Meditation(AA) state.
builds.other_notes: Rina is not a teammate for Yixuan as Sheer Damage ignores all DEF. All ATK buffers are good for Yixuan despite her scaling off of HP as Sheer converts 30% of your ATK into Sheer Force. For example Lucy's 600 ATK will convert to 180 Sheer Force. Nicole is suprisingly a strong support for Yixuan, as she gives CR% (M6), Ether DMG% Bonus (AA), and more DMG% from her Wengine.
builds.team_general: Any 2 Supports/Supportive agents or 1 Support + 1 Stunner to help out Yixuan's Damage

builds.other_notes: Despite her specialty in buffing Anomaly agents, she is still good for non-Anomaly teams because of the teamwide 1200 ATK and 15% DMG buff. If you have a higher refinement Weeping it can do better than Kaboom.
